parameters.content:
## 🔗 Integrations

Edit the config once, then attach your Telegram, Notion, weather, and Google Calendar inputs.

nodes.name: Sticky Note Integrations
nodes.notes: Section label for configuration and external integrations.

parameters.content:
## 📡 News Sources

The workflow fans out into independent fetch branches so one slow or broken source does not block the rest.

nodes.name: Sticky Note Sources
nodes.notes: Section label for the parallel fetch branches.

parameters.content:
## 🛠 Processing

Each branch formats its own Telegram-ready section before the multi-input merge.

nodes.name: Sticky Note Processing
nodes.notes: Section label for formatter, parser, and merge nodes.

parameters.content:
## 📤 Output

Merge, assemble, split for Telegram length limits, then send the final briefing.

nodes.name: Sticky Note Output
nodes.notes: Section label for merge, assembly, and Telegram delivery.

nodes.name: ⚙️ Config
nodes.notes:
👆 Edit these values to customize your briefing.
Set enable* to 'false' to skip any section.

nodes.name: ⏰ Morning Schedule
nodes.notes:
Change this cron to your preferred time.
Format: MM HH * * * (minute hour)
7:30 AM = 30 7 * * *
8:00 AM = 0 8 * * *

nodes.name: 🌤 Fetch Weather
nodes.notes: Fetches the current weather for the configured city. Set OPENWEATHER_API_KEY in n8n variables or environment if you don't attach a credential directly.

nodes.name: 🌤 Weather Error Pass-through
nodes.notes: Takes HTTP error output so the weather branch can still produce a null section.

nodes.name: 🌤 Format Weather
nodes.notes: Formats the weather response into a compact Telegram section.

nodes.name: 💻 Fetch HackerNews
nodes.notes: Fetches the current list of top Hacker News story IDs.

nodes.name: 💻 HN Error Pass-through
nodes.notes: Takes HTTP error output so the tech news branch still resolves cleanly.

nodes.name: 💻 Fetch HN Stories
nodes.notes: Fetches the top stories in batches and keeps the strongest technology headlines.

nodes.name: 🤖 Fetch ArXiv
nodes.notes: Fetches the latest ArXiv entries for AI and ML categories as raw XML text.

nodes.name: 🤖 ArXiv Error Pass-through
nodes.notes: Passes ArXiv HTTP errors forward so the parser can emit a null section instead of stopping.

nodes.name: 🤖 Parse ArXiv
nodes.notes: Pulls out the first ArXiv entry and formats a short AI paper summary.

nodes.name: 🔔 Fetch OpenAI RSS
nodes.notes: Reads the OpenAI blog RSS feed.

nodes.name: 🔔 Parse OpenAI RSS
nodes.notes: Formats the latest OpenAI RSS item as a single line.

nodes.name: 📋 Fetch Notion Tasks
nodes.notes: Fetches up to eight pages from your Notion task database. Replace the database ID and connect your Notion credential after import.

nodes.name: 📋 Format Notion Tasks
nodes.notes: Formats Notion database pages into a short task list and skips completed items when a status property exists.

nodes.name: 📅 Fetch Calendar
nodes.notes: Set GCAL_ICAL_URL in n8n Variables (Settings → Variables). Get it from Google Calendar → Settings → your calendar → Secret address in iCal format.

nodes.name: 📅 Calendar Error Pass-through
nodes.notes: Takes calendar HTTP errors so the formatter can return a clean null or fallback section.

nodes.name: 📅 Parse Calendar
nodes.notes: Extracts today's events from a Google Calendar iCal feed and formats them for Telegram.

nodes.name: 🗓 Build Timetable
nodes.notes: Provides a built-in editable timetable for student mode. Replace the sample schedule with your own classes.

nodes.name: 🔀 Merge All Sections
nodes.notes: Waits for all eight branches and emits one combined stream by position.

nodes.name: ✍️ Assemble Briefing
nodes.notes: Builds the final Telegram message, inserts dividers only where needed, and splits long messages into chunks.

nodes.name: 📨 Send to Telegram
nodes.notes: Connect your Telegram credential and replace the chat ID with your own personal chat or channel target.
